metadata.purpose: Pre-optimization baseline. Bench panel only, no Tasks 1-6 perf optimizations applied. Used as the 'before' half of the LC-1644 staging A/B comparison.
metadata.captured_by: AppEvent Perf Bench panel (Admin Tools tile)
metadata.trigger: wallet.invoke.benchAppEvent({...}) from the admin panel; results pasted from the panel's Copy Results JSON button
metadata.instrumentation_notes: On this branch handleSendCredentialEvent has no per-phase PerfTracker marks (Tasks 4/5 introduce them). Only sa_http_ms and sa_didauthvp_ms are populated from issueCredentialWithSigningAuthority's PerfTracker. Other phases (parallel_reads, owner_and_sa_reads, sa_issue, log_activity_and_send_boost) read 0 by design — their cost rolls up into total_ms.
metadata.posthog_status: Backend POSTHOG_API_KEY was not yet set on staging deploy when these runs were captured. Per-iteration / per-run events therefore did not arrive in PostHog for these specific runs. Frontend bench_appevent_run_triggered events did arrive (frontend uses posthog-js with its own config). Subsequent runs after env var is set will populate PostHog.

mid_run_anomaly_examples: run 3 iter 6: 683ms (sa_http=92, sa_didauthvp=43) — partial cold path
run 6 iter 5: 1564ms (sa_http=51, sa_didauthvp=2) — non-SA spike, source unknown
observations: Warm-path p50 is rock-solid at ~560ms across 7 independent runs.
SA call is consistently fast (~50ms warm). The April-era multi-second cold-start tail for sa_http is gone — staging Lambda is much warmer now.
~510ms of warm total_ms is in non-instrumented phases (Neo4j reads + sendBoost + logActivity + certification). Tasks 4-6 target this directly.
sa_didauthvp on cold paths is highly variable (43-217ms) — that's the brain-side crypto/DID resolution cache initializing.
Run 6 iter 5's 1564ms spike with warm SA call shows that non-SA tail anomalies exist independently of Lambda cold starts — possibly Neo4j slow query, GC pause, or sendBoost cert reissue.

runs.label: run-1
runs.notes: warmup=2 absorbed cold start; this is warm-path-only data. p95 is warm-path noise, not a cold tail.

runs.label: run-2
runs.notes: Cold start at iter 0 (1097ms total, 242ms sa_http, 102ms sa_didauthvp). Iter 1 lukewarm (555ms).

runs.label: run-3
runs.notes: Heaviest cold start observed (1239ms total, 269ms sa_http, 217ms sa_didauthvp). Iter 1 lukewarm (704ms). Iter 6 mid-run anomaly: 683ms with sa_http=92, sa_didauthvp=43 (partial cold path — possibly Lambda routing scatter).

runs.label: run-5
runs.notes: Lightest cold start (912ms). Iter 1 NOT lukewarm (579ms — fully warm). Iters 2-9 are 30-50ms faster than other runs (~520ms vs ~570ms baseline). Hypothesis: ran shortly after a previous bench, so brain process kept Bolt connection pool / DID-Web caches warm via inter-run state.

runs.label: run-6
runs.notes: Cold iter 0 (1106ms). MID-RUN ANOMALY at iter 5: total=1564ms with sa_http=51 (warm) and sa_didauthvp=2 (warm) — ~1500ms went somewhere NOT in the SA call. Suspects: Neo4j slow query, brain GC pause, or sendBoost cert reissue (issueCertifiedBoost would call SA again, second call's time wouldn't show in our sa_http metric). The optimized branch's per-phase instrumentation should diagnose this.

runs.label: run-7
runs.notes: Cold iter 0 = 1228ms with sa_http=390ms (highest cold sa_http observed across 7 runs). Iters 6 and 7 show mildly elevated sa_http (97ms, 82ms vs warm ~50ms) — possibly Lambda routing scatter today.

comparison_targets.warm_steady_p50_target_optimized: Drop from ~560ms to ~350-450ms via Tasks 4 (parallel reads + parallel log/sendBoost) and 5 (LRU cache). Most of the ~510ms unaccounted warm time should compress.
comparison_targets.lukewarm_iter1_target: Should converge with warm steady on the optimized branch — Task 5 LRU populates on iter 0, eliminates the cache-warm-up penalty visible on iter 1 here.
comparison_targets.cold_iter0_target: Modest improvement only — Lambda cold-start is staging-side. Task 3 (undici keepAlive + HTTP/2) may shave the TLS handshake portion. Don't expect a dramatic win on iter 0.
comparison_targets.tail_anomaly_target: Run 6 iter 5's 1500ms spike should be diagnosable on the optimized branch (which phase blew up?) and partially mitigated by Task 5's LRU cache (preventing repeat-read amplification).
